heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 13 May 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 0 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (213°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 1.6°C) and more moisture-laden rainfall (up 0.2 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 4095 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 2.3°C), outside temperature (up 2.2°C), inside temperature (up 4.3°C), and cloud levels (up 484 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 93.6m lower than yesterday, 307.1m lower than the past week, 484.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1275m to a maximum of 4370m, averaging 2400m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 2710m and 2890m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures may feel cool outdoors. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, low humidity will be noticeable and near calm conditions. Possible light showers, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The current weather conditions are not suitable as it is raining.
